items.title: Two-word spam email
items.scenario: Classes: spam vs ham. Features: x₁ = contains "FREE", x₂ = contains "!!!". Training estimates P(x₁|y) and P(x₂|y) separately. A new email has both flags set.
items.explanation: Under naive Bayes, P(x|spam) = P(FREE=1|spam) × P(!!!=1|spam). Multiply by P(spam) and compare to the ham product. No joint table P(FREE, !!!|y) is needed—only four per-feature probabilities per class.

items.title: Medical symptom screening
items.scenario: Disease y ∈ {flu, cold}. Symptoms x₁ = fever, x₂ = cough, x₃ = body ache—each binary. A patient presents fever=yes, cough=yes, ache=no.
items.explanation: Naive Bayes scores P(flu|x) ∝ P(flu) × P(fever|flu) × P(cough|flu) × P(no ache|flu), and similarly for cold. The model ignores correlations like fever+cough co-occurring more under flu; it still gives a fast triage score when data is limited.

items.title: Iris flower species (continuous features)
items.scenario: Class y is iris species. Features x₁ = sepal length, x₂ = sepal width—modeled as conditionally independent Gaussians per class.
items.explanation: P(x|y) = P(x₁|y) × P(x₂|y) where each P(xᵢ|y) is a normal density. Even though sepal length and width correlate in nature, Gaussian NB treats them as independent given species, estimating mean and variance per (feature, class) pair.

items.title: LLM prompt safety triage
items.scenario: y = {safe, policy-violating}. Features: x₁ = toxicity score bucket, x₂ = jailbreak keyword hit, x₃ = PII pattern detected. A flagged prompt has all three active.
items.explanation: Production filters often use Naive Bayes-style scoring: multiply class prior P(violating) by per-feature likelihoods learned from labeled prompts. Independence lets teams add new feature detectors without re-estimating full joint distributions.
